004 * this work for additional information regarding copyright ownership.
005 * The ASF licenses this file to You under the Apache License, Version 2.0
006 * (the "License"); you may not use this file except in compliance with

008 *

010 *
011 * Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
012 * distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
013 * WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
014 * See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
015 * limitations under the License.
016 */
017package org.apache.juneau.commons.reflect;
018
019import static org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.Utils.*;
020
021import java.lang.reflect.*;
022import java.util.*;
023
024import org.apache.juneau.commons.inject.*;
025
026/**
027 * Utility class providing convenient static methods for creating reflection info objects.
028 *
029 * <p>
030 * This class provides static factory methods that convert standard Java reflection objects
031 * ({@link Class}, {@link Method}, {@link Field}, {@link Constructor}) to their corresponding
032 * info wrapper objects ({@link ClassInfo}, {@link MethodInfo}, {@link FieldInfo}, {@link ConstructorInfo}).
033 *
034 * <h5 class='section'>Features:</h5>
035 * <ul class='spaced-list'>
036 *    <li>Convenient factory methods - convert reflection objects to info wrappers
037 *    <li>Null-safe - methods handle <jk>null</jk> inputs gracefully
038 *    <li>Unified API - consistent method naming across all reflection types
039 * </ul>
040 *
041 * <h5 class='section'>Use Cases:</h5>
042 * <ul class='spaced-list'>
043 *    <li>Converting reflection objects to info wrappers in a consistent way
044 *    <li>Simplifying code that works with both reflection and info objects
045 *    <li>Providing a centralized location for reflection-to-info conversions
046 * </ul>
047 *
048 * <h5 class='section'>Usage:</h5>
049 * <p class='bjava'>
050 *    <jc>// Convert Class to ClassInfo</jc>
051 *    ClassInfo <jv>ci</jv> = ReflectionUtils.<jsm>info</jsm>(MyClass.<jk>class</jk>);
052 *
053 *    <jc>// Convert Method to MethodInfo</jc>
054 *    Method <jv>m</jv> = MyClass.<jk>class</jk>.getMethod(<js>"myMethod"</js>);
055 *    MethodInfo <jv>mi</jv> = ReflectionUtils.<jsm>info</jsm>(<jv>m</jv>);
056 *
057 *    <jc>// Convert Field to FieldInfo</jc>
058 *    Field <jv>f</jv> = MyClass.<jk>class</jk>.getField(<js>"myField"</js>);
059 *    FieldInfo <jv>fi</jv> = ReflectionUtils.<jsm>info</jsm>(<jv>f</jv>);
060 *
061 *    <jc>// Convert Constructor to ConstructorInfo</jc>
062 *    Constructor&lt;?&gt; <jv>c</jv> = MyClass.<jk>class</jk>.getConstructor();
063 *    ConstructorInfo <jv>ci2</jv> = ReflectionUtils.<jsm>info</jsm>(<jv>c</jv>);
064 * </p>
065 *
066 * <h5 class='section'>Null Handling:</h5>
067 * <p>
068 * All methods in this class handle <jk>null</jk> inputs gracefully, returning <jk>null</jk> if the
069 * input is <jk>null</jk>. This makes it safe to use in scenarios where reflection objects may be <jk>null</jk>.
070 *
071 * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
072 *    <li class='jc'>{@link ClassInfo} - Class introspection wrapper
073 *    <li class='jc'>{@link MethodInfo} - Method introspection wrapper
074 *    <li class='jc'>{@link FieldInfo} - Field introspection wrapper
075 *    <li class='jc'>{@link ConstructorInfo} - Constructor introspection wrapper
076 *    <li class='link'><a class="doclink" href="https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/JuneauCommonsReflection">Reflection Package</a>
077 * </ul>
078 */
079public class ReflectionUtils {
080
081   /**
082    * Prevents instantiation.
083    */
084   private ReflectionUtils() {}
085
086   /**
087    * Returns the {@link ClassInfo} wrapper for the specified class.
088    *
089    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
090    * <p class='bjava'>
091    *    ClassInfo <jv>ci</jv> = ReflectionUtils.<jsm>info</jsm>(MyClass.<jk>class</jk>);
092    * </p>
093    *
094    * @param o The class to wrap. Can be <jk>null</jk>.
095    * @return The {@link ClassInfo} wrapper, or <jk>null</jk> if the input is <jk>null</jk>.
096    */
097   public static final <T> ClassInfoTyped<T> info(Class<T> o) {
098      return ClassInfo.of(o);
099   }
100
101   /**
102    * Returns the {@link ConstructorInfo} wrapper for the specified constructor.
103    *
104    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
105    * <p class='bjava'>
106    *    Constructor&lt;?&gt; <jv>c</jv> = MyClass.<jk>class</jk>.getConstructor();
107    *    ConstructorInfo <jv>ci</jv> = ReflectionUtils.<jsm>info</jsm>(<jv>c</jv>);
108    * </p>
109    *
110    * @param o The constructor to wrap. Can be <jk>null</jk>.
111    * @return The {@link ConstructorInfo} wrapper, or <jk>null</jk> if the input is <jk>null</jk>.
112    */
113   public static final ConstructorInfo info(Constructor<?> o) {
114      return ConstructorInfo.of(o);
115   }
116
117   /**
118    * Returns the {@link FieldInfo} wrapper for the specified field.
119    *
120    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
121    * <p class='bjava'>
122    *    Field <jv>f</jv> = MyClass.<jk>class</jk>.getField(<js>"myField"</js>);
123    *    FieldInfo <jv>fi</jv> = ReflectionUtils.<jsm>info</jsm>(<jv>f</jv>);
124    * </p>
125    *
126    * @param o The field to wrap. Can be <jk>null</jk>.
127    * @return The {@link FieldInfo} wrapper, or <jk>null</jk> if the input is <jk>null</jk>.
128    */
129   public static final FieldInfo info(Field o) {
130      return FieldInfo.of(o);
131   }
132
133   /**
134    * Returns the {@link MethodInfo} wrapper for the specified method.
135    *
136    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
137    * <p class='bjava'>
138    *    Method <jv>m</jv> = MyClass.<jk>class</jk>.getMethod(<js>"myMethod"</js>);
139    *    MethodInfo <jv>mi</jv> = ReflectionUtils.<jsm>info</jsm>(<jv>m</jv>);
140    * </p>
141    *
142    * @param o The method to wrap. Can be <jk>null</jk>.
143    * @return The {@link MethodInfo} wrapper, or <jk>null</jk> if the input is <jk>null</jk>.
144    */
145   public static final MethodInfo info(Method o) {
146      return MethodInfo.of(o);
147   }
148
149   /**
150    * Returns the {@link ClassInfo} wrapper for the class of the specified object.
151    *
152    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
153    * <p class='bjava'>
154    *    MyClass <jv>obj</jv> = <jk>new</jk> MyClass();
155    *    ClassInfo <jv>ci</jv> = ReflectionUtils.<jsm>info</jsm>(<jv>obj</jv>);
156    * </p>
157    *
158    * @param o The object whose class to wrap. Can be <jk>null</jk>.
159    * @return The {@link ClassInfo} wrapper for the object's class, or <jk>null</jk> if the input is <jk>null</jk>.
160    */
161   public static final ClassInfo info(Object o) {
162      return ClassInfo.of(o);
163   }
164
165   /**
166    * Converts a class to an enum class, suppressing rawtypes warnings.
167    *
168    * <p>
169    * This utility method wraps {@link Class#asSubclass(Class)} for enum classes and suppresses
170    * the rawtypes warning that occurs when converting to {@code Class<? extends Enum>} for use
171    * with methods like {@link EnumSet#allOf(Class)}.
172    *
173    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
174    * <p class='bjava'>
175    *    <jc>// Instead of:</jc>
176    *    <ja>@SuppressWarnings</ja>(<js>"rawtypes"</js>)
177    *    Class&lt;? <jk>extends</jk> Enum&gt; <jv>enumClass</jv> = <jv>clazz</jv>.asSubclass(Enum.<jk>class</jk>);
178    *
179    *    <jc>// Use:</jc>
180    *    Class&lt;? <jk>extends</jk> Enum&gt; <jv>enumClass</jv> = ReflectionUtils.<jsm>asEnumClass</jsm>(<jv>clazz</jv>);
181    * </p>
182    *
183    * @param clazz The class to convert. Must be an enum class.
184    * @return The enum class as a raw type suitable for use with {@link EnumSet#allOf(Class)}.
185    * @throws ClassCastException if the class is not an enum class.
186    */
187   @SuppressWarnings({
188      "rawtypes" // Raw types necessary for enum class return
189   })
190   public static Class<? extends Enum> asEnumClass(Class<?> clazz) {
191      return clazz.asSubclass(Enum.class);
192   }
193
194   /**
195    * Resolves and creates a collection, array, or map containing all beans of the element type from the bean store.
196    *
197    * <p>
198    * This is a helper method used internally by {@link ParameterInfo} and {@link FieldInfo} for resolving collection values.
199    *
200    * @param elementType The element type (for arrays/collections) or value type (for maps).
201    * @param beanStore The bean store to retrieve beans from.
202    * @param targetType The target collection type (must be a collection type).
203    * @return The collection, array, or map containing all beans of the element type, or <jk>null</jk> if not a supported collection type.
204    */
205   static Object resolveCollectionValue(Class<?> elementType, BeanStore beanStore, ClassInfo targetType) {
206      if (elementType == null || ! targetType.isInjectCollectionType())
207         return null;
208
209      // Get all beans of the element type
210      var beans = beanStore.getBeansOfType(elementType);
211
212      // Handle Map<String,T> - getBeansOfType already returns Map<String,T>
213      var inner = opt(targetType.inner()).orElse(Object.class);
214      if (Map.class.isAssignableFrom(inner)) {
215         // Verify it's Map<String,T> by checking the parameterized type
216         Type parameterizedType = targetType.innerType();
217         if (parameterizedType instanceof ParameterizedType pt2) {
218            var typeArgs = pt2.getActualTypeArguments();
219            if (typeArgs.length >= 2 && typeArgs[0] == String.class) {
220               return new LinkedHashMap<>(beans);
221            }
222         }
223         return null;
224      }
225
226      var values = new ArrayList<>(beans.values());
227
228      // Convert to appropriate collection/array type
229      if (targetType.isArray()) {
230         var array = Array.newInstance(elementType, values.size());
231         for (var i = 0; i < values.size(); i++)
232            Array.set(array, i, values.get(i));
233         return array;
234      }
235
236      if (eq(inner, Set.class)) {
237         return new LinkedHashSet<>(values);
238      } else if (eq(inner, List.class)) {
239         return values;
240      }
241
242      // Defensive check: return null if inner is null or not a supported collection type.
243      return null;
244   }
245}
